title: Cyclopentanone-2-Carboxyanilide as a Gravimetric Reagent for Mercury(II)
author: NIRMAL KUSUM CHAUDHURI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chemistry Department, The University of Burdwan, Burdwan-713101 

  Manuscript received 21 March 1975, revised 30 July 1975 ; accepted on 21 August 1975  

 Mercury (II) in quantities 25-40 mg has been determined gravimetrically by precipitating it as the cyclopentanone-2-carboxyanilide complex, Hg(C 12 H 12 O 2 N) 2,  containing 33.18% of the metal. The precipitation is quantitative in the  p H-range, 5.5-8.6. The complex can be weighed directly if it is dried below 105° for half an hour and then at 110°-115°. Mercury (II) has also been separated quantitatively from Cu 2+ , Cd 2+ , Zn 2+ , Co 2+ , Ni 2+ , Bi 3+ , Fe 3+ , and Al 3+ . In these separations citric acid-sodium citrate buffer acts as masking agent. The relative standard deviation is ± 0.32%.
